preamble: The short vowel /u/ reduces to schwa [ə] in unstressed or final syllables across multiple spoken Arabic dialects. This is particularly common in Levantine, Moroccan, and other varieties where short vowels undergo systematic merger with schwa in non-prominent positions.

examples.note: Complete merger of short /u/ with schwa in many Levantine dialects
Short /u/ merges with schwa in most contexts in Moroccan Arabic

context: Unstressed or final syllables; prominent in Levantine, Moroccan, and other spoken dialects
allophones_note: Short /u/ reduction to schwa in unstressed positions across multiple Arabic varieties
